summary: The articular disc of the temporomandibular joint is thickest posteriorly due to increased thickness from anterior (~2mm) to posterior (~3mm), narrows centrally owing to a thin central zone, and is functionally influenced by the lateral pterygoid muscle through direct contact and mechanical action affecting disc positioning.
formatted_summary: agent1 (Round 1 summarized): The articular disc of the temporomandibular joint is thickest posteriorly due to increased thickness from anterior (~2mm) to posterior (~3mm), narrows centrally owing to a thin central zone, and is functionally influenced by the lateral pterygoid muscle through direct contact and mechanical action affecting disc positioning.. Answer choice: D
